title: 10 Herbal Teas That Can Help with Hiatus Hernia Pain
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hiatus hernia are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of this condition by soothing the digestive system and reducing inflammation. The best herbal teas for hiatus hernia are Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Astragalus membranaceus, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber zerumbet, Lavandula angustifolia, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, hiatus hernia can lead to severe discomfort, acid reflux, and difficulty swallowing, severely impacting daily life, causing embarrassment, anxiety, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting one's overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help alleviate symptoms of hiatus hernia. When you have a hiatus hernia, stomach acid can flow back up into the esophagus, causing heartburn and discomfort. Ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues. It can also help slow down digestion, which can prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, ginger tea has a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help calm down the stomach and reduce symptoms of heartburn. This can make it easier to manage hiatus hernia symptoms and prevent further irritation of the esophagus. Drinking ginger tea after meals can also help reduce the risk of stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that can help with hiatus hernia, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers and boiling 1 liter of water in a large pot. Add 2-3 teaspoons of the dried flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers, and discard the solids. Allow the tea to cool down before drinking it, as it is best consumed when lukewarm or at room temperature. To use it for hiatus hernia, drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably before meals, to help soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you are taking medications or have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing herbal tea that can help alleviate hiatus hernia symptoms using Matricaria chamomilla, start by gathering a few fresh or dried chamomile flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently rinse them with clean water and pat dry with a paper towel. If using dried flowers, skip this step. Next, place a tablespoon of the chamomile flowers into a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Pour boiling water over the flowers in a cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. Once the tea has steeped, carefully remove the flowers or bag from the cup and let the tea cool down for a few minutes. Strain the tea into a clean cup if necessary, and sip it slowly while it's still warm. You can add a squeeze of lemon juice or a bit of honey to taste. Drink this tea 2-3 times a day to help ease hiatus hernia symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion.

supplementary_best: A combination of 'Curcuma longa', 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', and 'Foeniculum vulgare' teas may help alleviate hiatus hernia symptoms. 'Curcuma longa' or turmeric tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce irritation and inflammation in the stomach. 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' or licorice root tea has anti-ulcer properties, which can protect the stomach lining from acid damage. 'Foeniculum vulgare' or fennel tea is a natural carminative, which can ease digestive discomfort and bloating. This combination works well because it targets the root causes of hiatus hernia, including inflammation, acid damage, and digestive discomfort.  When consumed together, these teas may help reduce the frequency and severity of hiatus hernia symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of 'Curcuma longa' can reduce inflammation in the stomach, while 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' protects the stomach lining from acid damage. Meanwhile, 'Foeniculum vulgare' helps ease digestive discomfort and bloating, making it easier to manage hiatus hernia symptoms. This combination may be more effective than using any of these teas alone, as it addresses multiple aspects of the condition.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a hiatus hernia, it's best to avoid teas made from herbs that can irritate the stomach or make digestion worse. One such herb is 'Piper methysticum' (Kava). Kava can relax the lower esophageal sphincter, which is a muscle that helps keep food from coming back up into the esophagus. If this muscle relaxes too much, it can worsen hiatus hernia symptoms. Drinking kava tea regularly may cause more frequent and severe heartburn.  Another herb to avoid is 'Cassia auriculata' (Autumn crocus), also known as 'Cassia acutifolia'. This herb can cause stomach irritation and worsen digestive issues. Its alkaloids can also make the stomach produce more acid, which can irritate the esophagus and worsen hiatus hernia symptoms.  'Hedera helix' (English ivy) and 'Valeriana officinalis' (Valerian root) can also be problematic for people with hiatus hernia. English ivy can cause stomach cramps and worsen digestive issues, while Valerian root can slow down digestion, which can put more pressure on the stomach and worsen hiatus hernia symptoms.  Lastly, 'Lavandula angustifolia' (Lavender) and 'Schisandra chinensis' (Five-flavor berry) can be irritating to the stomach. While they may be calming and relaxing, they can still worsen hiatus hernia symptoms if consumed in tea form.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera juice is a natural remedy that can help with hiatus hernia. It's a soothing liquid that can calm the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. When we have a hiatus hernia, stomach acid often flows back up into the esophagus, causing pain and discomfort. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce this acid reflux. By drinking aloe vera juice, it can line the stomach and help prevent acid from flowing back up. Some people also find relief by taking digestive enzymes, like papain and bromelain, which help break down food in the stomach and reduce symptoms. Probiotics, which are live bacteria, can also be helpful as they promote a healthy balance of gut bacteria and can reduce inflammation in the stomach. These remedies work by addressing the underlying issues of a hiatus hernia, rather than just masking the symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with hiatus hernia often experience other health problems. One common issue is acid reflux, where stomach acid flows up into the esophagus, causing a burning sensation in the chest. This is because the hernia allows food to come back up, irritating the esophagus.  Some people with hiatus hernia also experience difficulty swallowing, known as dysphagia. This happens because the hernia can put pressure on the esophagus, making it harder for food to pass through.  Another common issue is heartburn, which is similar to acid reflux but occurs more frequently and can be more severe. Heartburn is caused by stomach acid flowing up into the esophagus, burning the lining and causing discomfort.  People with hiatus hernia may also experience bloating and discomfort in the stomach area. This is because the hernia can cause food to become trapped in the stomach, leading to gas and discomfort.  Some people may also experience stomach ulcers, which are open sores on the stomach lining. Stomach ulcers can be caused by the increased acid production due to the hernia, leading to pain and discomfort.  Certain medicinal herbs can help alleviate these symptoms. For example, slippery elm is a natural demulcent that can soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation. Licorice root is an anti-inflammatory that can help reduce acid production and soothe the stomach lining.  Ginger is another herb that can help alleviate symptoms. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach area.  Marshmallow root is also useful for soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation. It can help to coat the stomach and esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux.  Fennel seeds can also help to alleviate symptoms. They have carminative properties that can help to reduce gas and bloating in the stomach area.  Some medicinal herbs can also help to heal stomach ulcers. For example, aloe vera juice has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the stomach lining and promote healing.
